public_timeline_milestones: 2024-09-25 complaint/proposed-order package ; 2025-01-17 final decision and order ; 2025-02-11 final-order press release
exact_public_text_now: FTC complaint text says DoNotPay described the service as the world's first robot lawyer and an AI lawyer capable of drafting ironclad demand letters, contracts, small-claims complaints, and other legal-service tasks, while the final order bars representations that a covered product or service operates like a human lawyer by applying relevant laws to subscribers' legal and factual situations and records USD 193000 in monetary relief.
paper_safe_use_now: Strong candidate for a non-finance official AI-claim exemplar that can diversify shard-009 beyond settled SEC finance rows while staying at FTC complaint/final-order posture.
forbidden_overread: Do not restate this as a judicial unauthorized-practice finding, proof that the product actually functioned as claimed, or cross-sector prevalence evidence.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Promote first into the authoritative frontier and Table 1C diversification surfaces, then decide whether canonical case-ledger insertion is justified.
observatory_hook: nonfinance_official_ai_claim_anchor ; posture-aware AI-washing panel ; authority-depth funnel

public_timeline_milestones: 2025-04-28 proposed-order package ; 2025-08-28 final decision and order ; 2025-08-28 final-order press release
exact_public_text_now: FTC complaint text says Workado claimed its AI Content Detector would predict with 98.3% accuracy whether text was generated using AI systems such as ChatGPT, GPT4, Claude, and Bard, while the complaint states the model had not been tested for the plain-language text users typically submit and that published results showed substantially lower performance than 98.3%; the final order and customer-notice text preserve the false-or-unsubstantiated-accuracy framing and require notice plus claim substantiation controls.
paper_safe_use_now: Strong candidate for an official detector-accuracy / false-capability row that can bridge AI-washing with detector-governance and research-integrity discussion.
forbidden_overread: Do not convert FTC wording into a general scientific-validity conclusion about all AI detectors, nor into prevalence claims about detector fraud outside this bounded order.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Promote into diversification and detector-governance planning surfaces before any broader taxonomy or research-integrity rhetoric is strengthened.
